text: Bore in u creates a steep gradient. Pure spectral on u will Gibbs-oscillate. Try MUSCL-Godunov on u + Madelung-Psi on (N, phi). Does this split work? At what time does the soliton encounter the bore?

method: All-spectral RK4 on (u, N, phi). Negative control: expect Gibbs oscillations at bore and possibly Madelung/HJ failure since (sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N) blows up if N -> 0.

diagnostics.failure_location: HJ phi-equation, (sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N) at soliton tails

rationale: All-spectral fails immediately (t=1e-3, one step into the run) because the soliton tails make N ~ 1e-20 in the bulk, so the spectral d2x_spec(sqrt N)/(2 sqrt N) quantum pressure overflows to NaN. The bore in u also produces a small 0.26% overshoot above 1.0 (Gibbs onset) within the first sub-step, but the dominant failure mode is the HJ quantum pressure singularity. This confirms the Madelung formulation in (N, phi) is intrinsically fragile when N -> 0 anywhere in the domain.
Both pain points need separate cures: the bore in u needs shock-capturing (MUSCL+Godunov), and the HJ/quantum-pressure singularity needs a Madelung-Psi reformulation that never divides by sqrt(N).
next: E2 = MUSCL u + Madelung-Psi Strang split; also try E3 = MUSCL u + spectral (N, phi) as the discriminating control

method: MUSCL-Godunov SSP-RK3 on u (Burgers self-flux f=u^2/2 with van Leer slope limiter and Godunov upwind/sonic flux). Madelung Strang split on Psi = sqrt(N) e^{i phi}: cubic half-step (dealiased |psi|^2), linear dispersion full-step (spectral with 2/3 dealias), advection full-step (RK2 with u frozen), cubic half-step. Coupling: u -> Psi via advection u*Psi_x + (u_x/2)*Psi. FINAL CANDIDATE.

rationale: PASSES. Runs cleanly to T=8.0. MUSCL holds the bore monotone (zero overshoot/undershoot, TV(u) = 1.988 essentially equal to the strict TV bound of 2 for a step from 1 to 0). The bore rarefies as expected for u_t + u u_x = 0 with u_L=1>u_R=0: the foot (u=0.5 contour) moves from x=0 at t=0 to x=3.98 at t=8, matching the rarefaction characteristic speed (u+u)/2 = 0.5 in the fan plus the boost. The soliton drifts smoothly from x=-8 to x=+4.8 maintaining N_max in [0.993, 1.012] (less than 2% modulation). Madelung-Psi conserves |Psi|^2 mass to 1.5e-7 pre-collision and 7e-5 over the full run, which is the gold standard. Single peak (n_peaks_final = 1) confirms the soliton survives the bore traversal as a coherent transmission, NOT as reflection or capture. The soliton crosses x=0 (initial bore center) at t ~ 5.0; the rarefaction fan does not block it. Bore-soliton interaction is mild precisely because Madelung handles the cubic focusing exactly via the pointwise phase exponential, decoupling collapse-prone dynamics from the spectral linear step.

method: MUSCL-Godunov SSP-RK3 on u, but spectral SSP-RK3 on (N, phi) keeping the HJ form with quantum pressure (sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N). Discriminating control: tests whether shock-capturing on u alone is enough (it is not, if (N, phi) is not Madelung-transformed).

rationale: FAILS, with the same HJ quantum-pressure blow-up as E1, despite the MUSCL+Godunov on u running perfectly (zero overshoot). This proves the bore-related Gibbs is NOT the dominant failure mode; the HJ singularity (sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N) is. The conclusion is: BOTH cures are needed -- MUSCL on u to control the bore, AND Madelung-Psi on (N, phi) to avoid dividing by sqrt(N). Either alone is insufficient.

Three experiments have established a clean three-way comparison: E1 (no cure) fails at t=0.001; E3 (only u-cure) fails at t=0.006; E2 (both cures) passes through T=8 with 7e-5 mass drift. Knowledge captured in knowledge_findings.json. E2 = candidate.py.
